module_mood_contract: N/A — pre-engagement logistics email; no mood contract applies

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Classroom is the default delivery mode; the body teaches the work, the trainer manages the room.**

rules_evaluated.evidence: 25: 'The desktop app is fine too if you prefer it, both can be used for any exercise.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Agency language is fine; the mild over-hedge was flagged by writing judge (non-blocking). Story judge: no paternalism, choice is respected.

rules_evaluated.evidence: All four checks reference real, existing surfaces: claude.ai plan page, claude.com/code install, student's own repo, corporate network. No phantom artifacts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — and forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: Four named checks + reply-to-email escalation are the forcing function. No scripted reactions. No authored feeling-prompts. Deadline urgency ('do that today') is a forcing-function move, not a scripted reaction.
Not a lecture. No pre-exercise dosage concern.
No fenced prompt blocks in this artefact.
Pre-engagement email; no module mood contract applies per eval brief.

The email's framing of the four checks is practical, not corrective. 'Sorting these out today saves you (and me) the painful version of finding out 30 minutes into Module 1' is honest about stakes without implying the engineer has done something wrong.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: Subject line leads with action ('set up your laptop this week'). Body leads with what training IS ('hands-on from minute one'), not what breaks if unprepared.

Email establishes CLI-first default (consistent with AE101 strategy: 'terminal-comfortable' baseline). Real-repo requirement seeded ('one you own or co-own, where real work is happening'). Strategy doc alignment: real-work-only requirement present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Peer-register sim — does the engineer feel respected or talked down to?
rules_evaluated.evidence: Persona score 9/10. All four checks understood. Engineer will act. One mild register wobble in §2: parenthetical justifying CLI as 'engineer-native way' reads slightly defensive to a daily CLI user — minor. No preach, no consultant register detected.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Optional: cut 'it lives in your terminal, integrates with your editor and git, and is the engineer-native way to use Claude Code' from §2 parenthetical — peer doesn't need CLI justified. But this is non-blocking; current text passes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing-function adequacy — four checks as install-readiness gate
rules_evaluated.evidence: All four checks are concrete and actionable. Reply-to-email escalation is explicit. Urgency ('do that today') is distributed across checks without preachiness. No install rescue mid-Module-1 risk survives this email.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Hand-off to prework — does the email land where prework picks up?
rules_evaluated.evidence: 41: 'The prework itself lands in your inbox [X working days before Day 1], about 30 minutes of pre-session work, all of it in conversation with Claude.' Repo-candidate pre-think seeded in §3. Engineer arrives at prework with install done and repo shortlist ready.
